channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: MASEval: Extending Multi-Agent Evaluation from Models to Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.08835v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid adoption of LLM-based agentic systems has produced a rich ecosystem of frameworks (smolagents, LangGraph, AutoGen, CAMEL, LlamaIndex, i.a.). Yet existing benchmarks are model-centric: they fix the agentic setup and do not compare other...

item.title: LDP: An Identity-Aware Protocol for Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.08852v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As multi-agent AI systems grow in complexity, the protocols connecting them constrain their capabilities. Current protocols such as A2A and MCP do not expose model-level properties as first-class primitives, ignoring properties fundamental to...

item.title: Quantifying the Accuracy and Cost Impact of Design Decisions in Budget-Constrained Agentic LLM Search
item.description: arXiv:2603.08877v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems combine iterative search, planning prompts, and retrieval backends, but deployed settings impose explicit budgets on tool calls and completion tokens. We present a controlled measurement study of...

item.title: Interpretable Markov-Based Spatiotemporal Risk Surfaces for Missing-Child Search Planning with Reinforcement Learning and LLM-Based Quality Assurance
item.description: arXiv:2603.08933v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The first 72 hours of a missing-child investigation are critical for successful recovery. However, law enforcement agencies often face fragmented, unstructured data and a lack of dynamic, geospatial predictive tools. Our system, Guardian, provides an...

item.title: AgentOS: From Application Silos to a Natural Language-Driven Data Ecosystem
item.description: arXiv:2603.08938v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid emergence of open-source, locally hosted intelligent agents marks a critical inflection point in human-computer interaction. Systems such as OpenClaw demonstrate that Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents can autonomously operate local...

item.title: Equitable Multi-Task Learning for AI-RANs
item.description: arXiv:2603.08717v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI-enabled Radio Access Networks (AI-RANs) are expected to serve heterogeneous users with time-varying learning tasks over shared edge resources. Ensuring equitable inference performance across these users requires adaptive and fair learning...

item.title: Hindsight Credit Assignment for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.08754v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents often face significant credit assignment challenges in long-horizon, multi-step tasks due to sparse rewards. Existing value-free methods, such as Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), encounter two fundamental...

item.title: Generalized Reduction to the Isotropy for Flexible Equivariant Neural Fields
item.description: arXiv:2603.08758v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many geometric learning problems require invariants on heterogeneous product spaces, i.e., products of distinct spaces carrying different group actions, where standard techniques do not directly apply. We show that, when a group $G$ acts transitively...

item.title: SPREAD: Subspace Representation Distillation for Lifelong Imitation Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.08763v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A key challenge in lifelong imitation learning (LIL) is enabling agents to acquire new skills from expert demonstrations while retaining prior knowledge. This requires preserving the low-dimensional manifolds and geometric structures that underlie...

item.title: Multi-level meta-reinforcement learning with skill-based curriculum
item.description: arXiv:2603.08773v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We consider problems in sequential decision making with natural multi-level structure, where sub-tasks are assembled together to accomplish complex goals. Systematically inferring and leveraging hierarchical structure has remained a longstanding...

item.title: One Language, Two Scripts: Probing Script-Invariance in LLM Concept Representations
item.description: arXiv:2603.08869v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Do the features learned by Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) represent abstract meaning, or are they tied to how text is written? We investigate this question using Serbian digraphia as a controlled testbed: Serbian is written interchangeably in Latin and...

item.title: MultiGraSCCo: A Multilingual Anonymization Benchmark with Annotations of Personal Identifiers
item.description: arXiv:2603.08879v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accessing sensitive patient data for machine learning is challenging due to privacy concerns. Datasets with annotations of personally identifiable information are crucial for developing and testing anonymization systems to enable safe data sharing...

item.title: ConFu: Contemplate the Future for Better Speculative Sampling
item.description: arXiv:2603.08899v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding has emerged as a powerful approach to accelerate large language model (LLM) inference by employing lightweight draft models to propose candidate tokens that are subsequently verified by the target model. The effectiveness of this...

item.title: SciTaRC: Benchmarking QA on Scientific Tabular Data that Requires Language Reasoning and Complex Computation
item.description: arXiv:2603.08910v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce SciTaRC, an expert-authored benchmark of questions about tabular data in scientific papers requiring both deep language reasoning and complex computation. We show that current state-of-the-art AI models fail on at least 23% of these...

item.title: Automated Thematic Analysis for Clinical Qualitative Data: Iterative Codebook Refinement with Full Provenance
item.description: arXiv:2603.08989v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Thematic analysis (TA) is widely used in health research to extract patterns from patient interviews, yet manual TA faces challenges in scalability and reproducibility. LLM-based automation can help, but existing approaches produce codebooks with...

item.title: Alibaba Cloud to Build Hyperscale Computing Center in Shanghai’s Jinshan District
item.description: Alibaba Cloud will build a hyperscale AI computing center in Shanghai’s Jinshan District, deploying Pingtouge’s Xuantie chips to create a full-stack domestic computing infrastructure.

item.title: Zhipu AI Launches AutoClaw, a One-Click Local OpenClaw That Turns PCs Into 24/7 AI Agents
item.description: Zhipu AI launched AutoClaw, a one-click local OpenClaw installation that turns personal computers into 24/7 AI agents capable of automating complex workflows.

item.title: BYD and NIO Respond to Ongoing Debate Over EV Charging vs. Battery Swapping
item.description: BYD’s breakthrough ultra-fast charging technology and NIO’s expanding battery-swapping network have reignited the EV industry’s debate over charging versus swapping—while both sides increasingly signal coexistence.

item.title: Baidu’s Apollo Go Resumes Fully Driverless Testing and Operations in Dubai and Abu Dhabi
item.description: Baidu’s Apollo Go has resumed fully driverless operations in Dubai and Abu Dhabi, accelerating its push toward large-scale robotaxi deployment in the UAE.

item.title: OPPO to Raise Prices on Some Existing Smartphones as Memory Costs Surge
item.description: Rising AI-driven memory costs are forcing Chinese smartphone makers, including OPPO, to raise device prices in what could become the industry’s largest price hike in five years.

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from...

item.title: Increased errors with ChatGPT file downloads
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified that users are experiencing issues with downloading files from ChatGPT

item.title: Increased errors on ChatGPT File Uploads
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components File uploads (Degraded performance)

item.title: Zero Trust Dashboard UI: Issues creating Tunnels with Zero Trust Edit role
item.description: Mar 10, 20:15 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 10, 19:56 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating permission issues within the Zero Trust Dashboard UI where customers assigned the Zero Trust Edit role are unable to create Cloudflare Tunnels....

item.title: How NVIDIA Builds Open Data for AI
Microsoft Patches 83 Vulnerabilities
item.description: Microsoft has fixed a critical vulnerability, but none of the flaws fixed this Patch Tuesday has been exploited in the wild. The post Microsoft Patches 83 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Degraded performance with the Support chat
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe are still implementing the mitigation. As a temporary solution, customers looking to reach out to OpenAI Support can email support@openai.com "support@openai.com": mailto:support@openai.com.

item.title: Adobe Patches 80 Vulnerabilities Across Eight Products
item.description: Adobe has rolled out patches for 80 vulnerabilities across 8 products, including Commerce, Illustrator, Acrobat Reader, and Premiere Pro. The post Adobe Patches 80 Vulnerabilities Across Eight Products appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Jazz Emerges From Stealth With $61M in Funding for AI-Powered DLP
item.description: The startup brings AI to data loss prevention to provide visibility into intent, context, and risk. The post Jazz Emerges From Stealth With $61M in Funding for AI-Powered DLP appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Issue solving Challenge Pages
item.description: Mar 10, 16:45 UTCResolved - Some visitors may have had issues solving Challenge Pages, unable to complete the challenge successfully

item.title: FortiGate Devices Exploited to Breach Networks and Steal Service Account Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to a new campaign where threat actors are abusing FortiGate Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) appliances as entry points to breach victim networks.  The activity involves the exploitation of recently disclosed security vulnerabilities or weak...

item.title: KadNap Malware Infects 14,000+ Edge Devices to Power Stealth Proxy Botnet
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new malware called KadNap that's primarily targeting Asus routers to enlist them into a botnet for proxying malicious traffic. The malware, first detected in the wild in August 2025, has expanded to over 14,000 infected devices, with more than 60% of...

item.title: Kai Emerges From Stealth With $125M in Funding for AI Platform Bridging IT and OT Security
item.description: The company was created by a Claroty founder and is backed by Evolution Equity Partners, N47, and other investors. The post Kai Emerges From Stealth With $125M in Funding for AI Platform Bridging IT and OT Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Webinar Today: Securing Fragile OT in an Exposed World
item.description: Join the webinar as we examine the current OT threat landscape and move past the "doom and gloom" to discuss the mechanics of modern OT exposure. The post Webinar Today: Securing Fragile OT in an Exposed World appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Browser-based RDP experiencing issues with Firefox
item.description: Mar 10, 15:04 UTCResolved - An interim solution has been identified. Users can open a new tab in Firefox, enter `about:config`, and locate the `dom.events.testing.asyncClipboard` setting, and set it to `true`.Mar 10, 13:28 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an issue where browser-based...

item.title: Access Configuration Updates Delayed
item.description: Mar 10, 14:49 UTCInvestigating - There are delays with Access configuration changes making it to the edge. Existing configuration and logins are not impacted.

item.title: How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect view of the world
item.description: Pokémon Go was the world’s first augmented-reality megahit. Released in 2016 by the Google spinout Niantic, the AR twist on the juggernaut Pokémon franchise fast became a global phenomenon. From Chicago to Oslo to Enoshima, players hit the streets in the urgent hope of catching a Jigglypuff or a...

item.title: New "LeakyLooker" Flaws in Google Looker Studio Could Enable Cross-Tenant SQL Queries
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed nine cross-tenant vulnerabilities in Google Looker Studio that could have permitted attackers to run arbitrary SQL queries on victims' databases and exfiltrate sensitive data within organizations' Google Cloud environments. The shortcomings have been...

item.title: Active – Azure OpenAI Service request failures in multiple regions
item.description: Impact Statement: Starting at 06:35 UTC on 10 March 2026, a subset of customers using Azure OpenAI Service in multiple regions may experience intermittent request failures or reduced availability.Current Status: We detected this issue through automated service monitoring after observing an elevated...

item.title: Prioritizing energy intelligence for sustainable growth
item.description: Loudoun County, Virginia, once known for its pastoral scenery and proximity to Washington, DC, has earned a more modern reputation in recent years: The area has the highest concentration of data centers on the planet. Ten years ago, these facilities powered email and e-commerce. Today, thanks to...

item.title: The Download: AI’s role in the Iran war, and an escalating legal fight
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How AI is turning the Iran conflict into theater  Much of the spotlight on AI in the Iran conflict has focused on models like Claude...

item.title: How to Stop AI Data Leaks: A Webinar Guide to Auditing Modern Agentic Workflows
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer just a tool we talk to; it is a tool that does things for us. These are called AI Agents. They can send emails, move data, and even manage software on their own. But there is a problem. While these agents make work faster, they also open a new "back door"...

item.title: The Zero-Day Scramble is Avoidable: A Guide to Attack Surface Reduction
item.description: You can't control when the next critical vulnerability drops. You can control how much of your environment is exposed when it does. The problem is that most teams have more internet-facing exposure than they realise. Intruder's Head of Security digs into why this happens and how teams can manage it...

item.title: NVIDIA's AI Engineers: Agent Inference at Planetary Scale and "Speed of Light" — Nader Khalil (Brev), Kyle Kranen (Dynamo)
item.description: NVIDIA welcomes AI Engineers with a special pre GTC episode!

item.title: [AINews] Autoresearch: Sparks of Recursive Self Improvement
item.description: AGI takes another small step forward.

item.title: Deep Research requests are erroring for LATAM users
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Deep Research (Operational)

item.title: Keep the Tokens Flowing: Lessons from 16 Open-Source RL Libraries
Introducing Storage Buckets on the Hugging Face Hub
Granite 4.0 1B Speech: Compact, Multilingual, and Built for the Edge
Evaluating Financial Intelligence in Large Language Models: Benchmarking SuperInvesting AI with LLM Engines
item.description: Large language models are increasingly used for financial analysis and investment research, yet systematic evaluation of their financial reasoning capabilities remains limited. In this work, we introduce the AI Financial Intelligence Benchmark (AFIB), a multi-dimensional evaluation framework...
